message:
fix(security): remove command injection vulnerability in validate_directory

The validate_directory() function in newproj_errors.sh used `eval echo "$dir"`
to expand user-provided directory paths. This allowed arbitrary command
execution if a user entered a malicious path like `$(rm -rf /)` or
`\`malicious_command\``.

Root cause: The developer wanted to expand ~ (tilde) to the user's home
directory, but used eval which executes arbitrary shell commands.

Fix: Replace eval-based expansion with safe pattern matching that only
handles the tilde cases:
- "~" -> $HOME
- "~/path" -> $HOME/path
- Other inputs (including ~user) pass through unchanged

This is a security-critical fix as the function is called from the TUI
wizard where users enter directory paths interactively.

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -593,9 +593,17 @@ validate_directory() {
     local dir="$1"
     local error_msg=""
 
-    # Expand path
+    # Expand tilde to home directory (safe, no eval)
+    # Handles: ~, ~/path, ~user (falls back to literal for ~user)
     local expanded_dir
-    expanded_dir=$(eval echo "$dir" 2>/dev/null) || expanded_dir="$dir"
+    if [[ "$dir" == "~" ]]; then
+        expanded_dir="$HOME"
+    elif [[ "$dir" == "~/"* ]]; then
+        expanded_dir="${HOME}${dir:1}"
+    else
+        # No tilde expansion needed (or ~user form which we don't expand for security)
+        expanded_dir="$dir"
+    fi
 
     # Check if path is absolute or can be made absolute
     if [[ "$expanded_dir" != /* ]]; then
